headline: Phillips 66 Weighs Venezuelan Crude Sourcing Shift And Margin Trade Offs
summary: Phillips 66 (NYSE:PSX) is seeking regulatory approval to buy heavy crude directly from Venezuela’s state oil company PDVSA. The move would shift PSX from using intermediaries to dealing directly with PDVSA for certain crude supplies. Regulatory review will determine whether these direct purchases can proceed under current US rules. Phillips 66 is a major US refiner and midstream operator, with a business model that depends heavily on securing reliable and cost effective crude feedstock...

headline: Exxon Is Down Since the Iran Conflict. These Energy Stocks Are Bigger Winners.
summary: For all the drama in oil markets since the recent conflict in Iran began, stocks of U.S. oil majors such as  Exxon  Mobil—and even those of many oil producers—haven’t budged much.  In fact, Exxon stock is down from its Friday closing price.  The markets for the fuels those companies produce have been even more volatile than the overall oil market.

headline: Reddit Is Bullish on XLE But Crude Oil Tells a Different Story
summary: The State Street Energy Select Sector SPDR Fund (NYSEARCA:XLE), is up 27% year-to-date and 13% over the past month, trading at $56.78 Monday morning. The catalyst is unmistakable: Iran-Israel tensions have escalated sharply, with the U.S. ordering non-essential embassy staff out of Israel, a development the world is keeping a close eye on.
